diagrams.title: Data-Sharing Attributes Comparison Table
diagrams.description: Comprehensive table comparing all OpenMP data-sharing attributes with their characteristics, initialization, and use cases
elements: SHARED: shared(x) - copies from master, persists, all threads access same location
PRIVATE: private(x) - uninitialized, per region, thread-local storage
FIRSTPRIVATE: firstprivate(x) - initialized from master, per region, preserves initial value
LASTPRIVATE: lastprivate(x) - uninitialized, copy from last iteration, sequential final value
REDUCTION: reduction(+:x) - initialized to identity, combines at end, associative operators
THREADPRIVATE: threadprivate(x) - preserved across regions, static/file scope only
DEFAULT(none): forces explicit declaration for all variables
DEFAULT(shared): implicit shared (default behavior)
diagrams.purpose: Help students understand when to use each data-sharing attribute and avoid common parallel programming errors

diagrams.title: Race Condition Timing Diagram
diagrams.description: Gantt chart showing how two threads can cause lost updates when incrementing a shared counter concurrently
elements: Time axis showing sequential steps: READ, INCREMENT, WRITE
Thread 1: READ(x=5) → INCREMENT(6) → WRITE(x=6)
Thread 2: READ(x=5) → INCREMENT(6) → WRITE(x=6) [interleaved]
Final result: x=6 (should be 7)
Annotation showing 'LOST UPDATE' between Thread 1 and Thread 2 reads
Solution: Use reduction or atomic to serialize the read-modify-write operation
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the exact sequence of events that cause data races and understand why concurrent updates lose data

diagrams.title: Memory Layout for Shared vs Private Variables
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how shared and private variables are stored in memory during parallel execution
elements: Main Memory: Shared variables A and B stored in single location
Thread 1 Stack: Private copies x1, y1
Thread 2 Stack: Private copies x2, y2
Thread 3 Stack: Private copies x3, y3
Thread 4 Stack: Private copies x4, y4
Arrows showing threads reading/writing to shared memory
Dashed boxes showing thread-local private storage areas
Annotation: 'Private variables have separate copies per thread'
diagrams.purpose: Reinforce the conceptual model of variable scoping in parallel memory architectures
